game_info.title: Tattletail - Horror Game
game_info.concept:
It’s Christmas 1998. The world’s most annoying toy just came out, and yours won’t stay in his box. Can you stop Tattletail from getting you in trouble before the bloodthirsty Mama Tattletail hunts you down? Story-driven first person horror. New DLC Included!

New DLC Campaign Included!

Purchase Tattletail and get The Kaleidoscope Expansion DLC for free! The Kaleidoscope is a whole new campaign that unlocks after you complete the original game. It tells a very different story about the events of Christmas 1998, starring a strange green-eyed Tattletail. New nights, new scares, and mysterious consequences.


About the Game

Who remembers Talking Tattletail? It was SO creepy, right??

There’s not much reference online but the original version, Mama Tattletail, was recalled years earlier… and I’m pretty sure I know why.

90's Virtual Pet Horror

Tattletail is a short, story-driven first person horror game about everyone's favorite talking toy from the 90's. You opened your Tattletail before Christmas, and now he won't stay in his box. Can you keep him out of trouble until Christmas day? Or will Mama Tattletail find her baby and hunt you down first?
  • Obey Tattletail's mischevious demands.
  • Feed, Brush, and Recharge Tattletail... or he'll never shut up.
  • Shake your flashlight to charge it... but be careful. Someone might hear.
  • Do not wake up Mama Tattletail
  • DO NOT MAKE LOUD NOISES NEAR MAMA TATTLETAIL

This is our first game as Waygetter Electronics. We'd love to hear your feedback!!
Please report all sightings of Mama Tattletail to Waygetter Electronics
game_info.description: It's Christmas Eve 1998, and you've snuck downstairs to play with your new Tattletail toy early. But this annoying virtual pet won't stop making noise and demanding attention! Keep Tattletail fed, brushed, and charged while exploring your house in first-person view. But beware - if you make too much noise or use your flashlight carelessly, the terrifying Mama Tattletail will hunt you down. Survive until morning by managing your resources and keeping quiet. The house is dark, your flashlight battery is limited, and Mama is always listening...
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move forward/backward and turn left/right
Space: Interact with Tattletail (feed/brush/charge)
Shift: Shake flashlight to recharge battery
Z: Toggle flashlight on/off

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement and navigation through the house. Verify player can move in all directions, turn properly, and navigate between rooms without getting stuck. Ensure collision detection works with walls and obstacles.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move through all rooms systematically using arrow keys. Test forward, backward, and rotation. Navigate through doorways and around furniture. Verify boundaries prevent leaving the playable area.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully navigates entire house without clipping through walls. All rooms are accessible. Movement feels responsive and collision detection prevents invalid positions. Test passes when full house exploration is complete.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing Tattletail interaction system and resource management. Verify feeding, brushing, and charging mechanics work correctly. Ensure Tattletail's needs decrease over time and can be satisfied through proper interaction.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Locate Tattletail and cycle through all interaction types using Space key. Monitor Tattletail's status bars for hunger, cleanliness, and battery. Perform each action multiple times to refill meters. Test noise generation from interactions.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All three care actions (feed, brush, charge) successfully increase corresponding meters. Tattletail stops making noise when needs are met. Noise level increases when Tattletail is unhappy. Test succeeds when all meters are maintained above critical levels.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing flashlight mechanics including battery drain, recharge via shaking, and light radius. Verify flashlight toggle works and that shaking generates noise that can attract Mama Tattletail.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Toggle flashlight on/off repeatedly with Z key. Let battery drain while light is on. Use Shift key to shake and recharge battery. Test in different rooms to verify light radius and shadow effects.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Flashlight turns on/off correctly. Battery drains while active and recharges when shaking. Shaking generates noise visible in noise meter. Light provides adequate visibility in dark rooms. Test passes when full battery cycle is demonstrated.
TEST_3.description: Testing flashlight mechanics including battery drain, recharge via shaking, and light radius. Verify flashlight toggle works and that shaking generates noise that can attract Mama Tattletail.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing Mama Tattletail AI behavior and hunting mechanics. Verify she spawns when noise threshold is exceeded, hunts player effectively, and can catch player for game over condition.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Intentionally make excessive noise by running, using flashlight carelessly, and ignoring Tattletail's needs. Monitor for Mama's spawn and approach. Test if she follows player through rooms and catches player when too close.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Mama spawns after noise threshold exceeded. She actively hunts player position. Audio cues indicate her proximity. Getting caught triggers game over screen. Test succeeds when Mama's full behavior cycle is triggered and game over condition is reached.
TEST_4.description: Testing Mama Tattletail AI behavior and hunting mechanics. Verify she spawns when noise threshold is exceeded, hunts player effectively, and can catch player for game over condition.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing stealth mechanics and noise reduction strategies. Verify that moving slowly, keeping flashlight off, and satisfying Tattletail keeps noise low and prevents Mama from spawning.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Play cautiously by moving slowly, minimizing flashlight use, and quickly responding to Tattletail's needs. Keep noise meter in safe zone. Test if staying quiet prevents Mama encounters over extended play session.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Noise meter remains low when playing carefully. Mama doesn't spawn when noise is managed properly. Player can survive multiple minutes without encounter. Test passes when stealth gameplay prevents Mama spawn for significant duration.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing win condition - surviving until morning (timer reaches zero). Verify game transitions to victory screen when time runs out while player is still alive.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Use optimal strategy combining stealth, resource management, and efficient movement. Keep Tattletail satisfied, noise low, and avoid Mama. Navigate efficiently to minimize time exposed. Survive until timer countdown completes.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Timer counts down properly during gameplay. Upon reaching zero, game transitions to win screen. Victory message displays with final statistics. Test succeeds when win condition is reached through skillful play.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing complete game loop including restart functionality. Verify player can restart from game over or win screen, and game state resets properly for new playthrough.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Play through to either win or loss condition. Press R key at end screen to restart. Verify all game elements reset: player position, Tattletail status, Mama state, timer, and noise level. Start new game from beginning.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: R key successfully restarts game from any end state. All variables reset to initial values. Player returns to starting position. New game plays identically to first run. Test passes when multiple complete cycles can be played without issues.
